oracles.content: Qilin whispers: Ziqi Gu was once richer than empires… until he discovered Warhammer 40K. Plastic armies consumed his soul—and his savings.
Qilin hums softly: Ziqi Gu knows that with NYUSH VPN, one may stand in America yet binge anime on Bilibili. He calls this ‘academic dual citizenship.’
Qilin tilts its head: Ziqi Gu does not enjoy LeetCode. He does not deny it, nor confirm it. He simply stares into the void—and the void segfaults.
Qilin smiles: Ziqi Gu believes the Magnolia Café serves the best ice cream in the world. He has sacrificed both time and GPA to verify this claim.
Qilin sighs: Ziqi Gu remembers when the NYUSH library leaked from the heavens. He learned that knowledge is fluid—and so is the ceiling.
Qilin observes: Ziqi Gu hears CS students complain about everything—yet somehow, they still compile. He finds this both impressive and concerning.
Qilin recalls: Ziqi Gu once ran three research projects at the same time. He gained wisdom, lost sleep, and forgot what day it was.
Qilin chuckles: Ziqi Gu says he has no time—yet somehow scrolls endlessly. Even ancient beasts find this paradox fascinating.
Qilin warns: Ziqi Gu knows the bug you cannot find has already found you. It waits patiently, hidden in plain sight.
Qilin declares: Ziqi Gu is always closer to success than he thinks—but also closer to his next breakdown. This is called balance.
Qilin observes: Ziqi Gu achieved a respectable GPA. Unfortunately, happiness was not included in the grading rubric.
Qilin nods: Ziqi Gu was once saved by an MTA worker. In a city that never stops, even strangers become temporary guardians.
Qilin sighs: Ziqi Gu failed two coding tests… and still does not understand heap lazy update. Persistence is admirable. Results are optional.
Qilin speaks softly: Ziqi Gu loves BanG Dream so deeply that he built a website to remember it. Some things in life fade quietly—but some, he chooses to preserve forever.
